title: Canto 10 Chapter 34
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचएकदा देवयात्रायां गोपाला जातकौतुका: ।अनोभिरनडुद्युक्तै: प्रययुस्तेऽम्बिकावनम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaekadā deva-yātrāyāṁgopālā jāta-kautukāḥanobhir anaḍud-yuktaiḥprayayus te ’mbikā-vanam
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: One day the cowherd men, eager to take a trip to worship Lord Śiva, traveled by bullock carts to the Ambikā forest.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; ekadā; deva; yatrāyām; gopālāḥ; jāta; kautukāḥ; anobhiḥ; anaḍut; yuktaiḥ; prayayuḥ; te; ambikā; vanam
verses.purport: According to Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, the word ekadā here indicates the occasion of Śiva-rātri. He further mentions that Ambikāvana is in Gujarat province, near the city of Siddhapura. Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura adds that the departure of the cowherd men specifically took place on the fourteenth lunar day of the dark fortnight of the month of Phālguna. Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī also quotes authorities who claim that Ambikāvana lies on the bank of the Sarasvatī River, northwest of Mathurā. Ambikāvana is notable because within it are deities of Śrī Śiva and his wife, goddess Umā.

verses.text: तत्र स्‍नात्वा सरस्वत्यां देवं पशुपतिं विभुम् ।आनर्चुरर्हणैर्भक्त्या देवीं च नृपतेऽम्बिकाम् ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra snātvā sarasvatyāṁdevaṁ paśu-patiṁ vibhumānarcur arhaṇair bhaktyādevīṁ ca ṇṛpate ’mbikām
verses.meaning: O King, after arriving there, they bathed in the Sarasvatī and then devotedly worshiped with various paraphernalia the powerful Lord Paśupati and his consort, goddess Ambikā.
verses.wordMeanings: tatra; snātvā; sarasvatyām; devam; paśu; patim; vibhum; ānarcuḥ; arhaṇaiḥ; bhaktyā; devīm; ca; nṛ; pate; ambikām

verses.text: गावो हिरण्यं वासांसि मधु मध्वन्नमाद‍ृता: ।ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददु: सर्वे देवो न: प्रीयतामिति ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāvo hiraṇyaṁ vāsāṁsimadhu madhv-annam ādṛtāḥbrāhmaṇebhyo daduḥ sarvedevo naḥ prīyatām iti
verses.meaning: The cowherd men gave the brāhmaṇas gifts of cows, gold, clothing and cooked grains mixed with honey. Then the cowherds prayed, “May the lord be pleased with us.”
verses.wordMeanings: gāvaḥ; hiraṇyam; vāsāṁsi; madhu; madhu; annam; ādṛtāḥ; brāhmaṇebhyaḥ; daduḥ; sarve; devaḥ; naḥ; prīyatām; iti

verses.text: ऊषु: सरस्वतीतीरे जलं प्राश्य यतव्रता: ।रजनीं तां महाभागा नन्दसुनन्दकादय: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ūṣuḥ sarasvatī-tīrejalaṁ prāśya yata-vratāḥrajanīṁ tāṁ mahā-bhāgānanda-sunandakādayaḥ
verses.meaning: Nanda, Sunanda and the other greatly fortunate cowherds spent that night on the bank of the Sarasvatī, strictly observing their vows. They fasted, taking only water.
verses.wordMeanings: ūṣuḥ; sarasvatī; tīre; jalam; prāśya; yata; vratāḥ; rajanīm; tām; mahā; bhāgāḥ; nanda; sunandaka; ādayaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains that Sunanda is the younger brother of Nanda Mahārāja.

verses.text: कश्चिन्महानहिस्तस्मिन् विपिनेऽतिबुभुक्षित: ।यद‍ृच्छयागतो नन्दं शयानमुरगोऽग्रसीत् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaścin mahān ahis tasminvipine ’ti-bubhukṣitaḥyadṛcchayāgato nandaṁśayānam ura-go ’grasīt
verses.meaning: During the night a huge and extremely hungry snake appeared in that thicket. Slithering on his belly up to the sleeping Nanda Mahārāja, the snake began swallowing him.
verses.wordMeanings: kaścit; mahān; ahiḥ; tasmin; vipine; ati; bubhukṣitaḥ; yadṛcchayā; āgataḥ; nandam; śayānam; ura; gaḥ; agrasīt

verses.text: स चुक्रोशाहिना ग्रस्त: कृष्ण कृष्ण महानयम् ।सर्पो मां ग्रसते तात प्रपन्नं परिमोचय ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cukrośāhinā grastaḥkṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahān ayamsarpo māṁ grasate tātaprapannaṁ parimocaya
verses.meaning: In the clutches of the snake, Nanda Mahārāja cried out, “Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa, my dear boy! This huge serpent is swallowing me! Please save me, who am surrendered to You!”
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; cukrośa; ahinā; grastaḥ; kṛṣṇa; kṛṣṇa; mahān; ayam; sarpaḥ; mām; grasate; tāta; prapannam; parimocaya

verses.text: तस्य चाक्रन्दितं श्रुत्वा गोपाला: सहसोत्थिता: ।ग्रस्तं च द‍ृष्ट्वा विभ्रान्ता: सर्पं विव्यधुरुल्मुकै: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya cākranditaṁ śrutvāgopālāḥ sahasotthitāḥgrastaṁ ca dṛṣṭvā vibhrāntāḥsarpaṁ vivyadhur ulmukaiḥ
verses.meaning: When the cowherd men heard the cries of Nanda, they immediately rose up and saw that he was being swallowed. Distraught, they beat the serpent with blazing torches.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; ca; ākranditam; śrutvā; gopālāḥ; sahasā; utthitāḥ; grastam; ca; dṛṣṭvā; vibhrāntāḥ; sarpam; vivyadhuḥ; ulmukaiḥ

verses.text: अलातैर्दह्यमानोऽपि नामुञ्चत्तमुरङ्गम: ।तमस्पृशत्पदाभ्येत्य भगवान्सात्वतां पति: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: alātair dahyamāno ’pināmuñcat tam uraṅgamaḥtam aspṛśat padābhyetyabhagavān sātvatāṁ patiḥ
verses.meaning: But even though the firebrands were burning him, the serpent would not release Nanda Mahārāja. Then the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa, master of His devotees, came to the spot and touched the snake with His foot.
verses.wordMeanings: alātaiḥ; dahyamānaḥ; api; na; amuñcat; tam; uraṅgamaḥ; tam; aspṛśat; padā; abhyetya; bhagavān; sātvatām; patiḥ

verses.text: स वै भगवत: श्रीमत्पादस्पर्शहताशुभ: ।भेजे सर्पवपुर्हित्वा रूपं विद्याधरार्चितम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vai bhagavataḥ śrīmatpāda-sparśa-hatāśubhaḥbheje sarpa-vapur hitvārūpaṁ vidyādharārcitam
verses.meaning: The snake had all his sinful reactions destroyed by the touch of the Supreme Lord’s divine foot, and thus he gave up his serpent body and appeared in the form of a worshipable Vidyādhara.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; vai; bhagavataḥ; śrī; mat; pāda; sparśa; hata; aśubhaḥ; bheje; sarpa; vapuḥ; hitvā; rūpam; vidyādhara; arcitam
verses.purport: The words rūpaṁ vidyādharārcitam indicate that the erstwhile snake appeared in a beautiful form worshipable among the demigods called Vidyādharas. In other words, he appeared as the leader of the Vidyādharas.

verses.text: तमपृच्छद् धृषीकेश: प्रणतं समवस्थितम् ।दीप्यमानेन वपुषा पुरुषं हेममालिनम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tam apṛcchad dhṛṣīkeśaḥpraṇataṁ samavasthitamdīpyamānena vapuṣāpuruṣaṁ hema-mālinam
verses.meaning: The Supreme Lord Hṛṣīkeśa then questioned this personality, who was standing before Him with his head bowed, his brilliantly effulgent body bedecked with golden necklaces.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; apṛcchat; hṛṣīkeśaḥ; praṇatam; samavasthitam; dīpyamānena; vapuṣā; puruṣam; hema; mālinam
verses.purport: The demigod was about to speak, and Lord Kṛṣṇa wanted to focus everyone’s attention on his words. Therefore He personally inquired from the worshipable Vidyādhara, who was standing before Him with his head bowed.

verses.text: को भवान् परया लक्ष्म्या रोचतेऽद्भ‍ुतदर्शन: ।कथं जुगुप्सितामेतां गतिं वा प्रापितोऽवश: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko bhavān parayā lakṣmyārocate ’dbhuta-darśanaḥkathaṁ jugupsitām etāṁgatiṁ vā prāpito ’vaśaḥ
verses.meaning: [Lord Kṛṣṇa said:] My dear sir, you appear so wonderful, glowing with such great beauty. Who are you? And who forced you to assume this terrible body of a snake?
verses.wordMeanings: kaḥ; bhavān; parayā; lakṣmyā; rocate; adbhuta; darśanaḥ; katham; jugupsitām; etām; gatim; vā; prāpitaḥ; avaśaḥ

verses.text: सर्प उवाचअहं विद्याधर: कश्चित्सुदर्शन इति श्रुत: ।श्रिया स्वरूपसम्पत्त्या विमानेनाचरन् दिश: ॥ १२ ॥ऋषीन् विरूपाङ्गिरस: प्राहसं रूपदर्पित: ।तैरिमां प्रापितो योनिं प्रलब्धै: स्वेन पाप्मना ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarpa uvācaahaṁ vidyādharaḥ kaścitsudarśana iti śrutaḥśriyā svarūpa-sampattyāvimānenācaran diśaḥ
verses.meaning: The serpent replied: I am the well-known Vidyādhara named Sudarśana. I was very opulent and beautiful, and I used to wander freely in all directions in my airplane. Once I saw some homely sages of the lineage of Aṅgirā Muni. Proud of my beauty, I ridiculed them, and because of my sin they made me assume this lowly form.
verses.wordMeanings: sarpaḥ; uvāca; aham; vidyādharaḥ; kaścit; sudarśanaḥ; iti; śrutaḥ; śriyā; svarūpa; sampattyā; vimānena; ācaran; diśaḥ; ṛṣīn; virūpa; āṅgirasaḥ; prāhasam; rūpa; darpitaḥ; taiḥ; imām; prāpitaḥ; yonim; pralabdhaiḥ; svena; pāpmanā

verses.text: शापो मेऽनुग्रहायैव कृतस्तै: करुणात्मभि: ।यदहं लोकगुरुणा पदा स्पृष्टो हताशुभ: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śāpo me ’nugrahāyaivakṛtas taiḥ karuṇātmabhiḥyad ahaṁ loka-guruṇāpadā spṛṣṭo hatāśubhaḥ
verses.meaning: It was actually for my benefit that those merciful sages cursed me, since now I have been touched by the foot of the supreme spiritual master of all the worlds and have thus been relieved of all inauspiciousness.
verses.wordMeanings: śāpaḥ; me; anugrahāya; eva; kṛtaḥ; taiḥ; karuṇa; ātmabhiḥ; yat; aham; loka; guruṇā; padā; spṛṣṭaḥ; hata; aśubhaḥ

verses.text: तं त्वाहं भवभीतानां प्रपन्नानां भयापहम् ।आपृच्छे शापनिर्मुक्त: पादस्पर्शादमीवहन् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tvāhaṁ bhava-bhītānāṁprapannānāṁ bhayāpahamāpṛcche śāpa-nirmuktaḥpāda-sparśād amīva-han
verses.meaning: My Lord, You destroy all fear for those who, fearing this material world, take shelter of You. By the touch of Your feet I am now freed from the curse of the sages. O destroyer of distress, please let me return to my planet.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; tvā; aham; bhava; bhītānām; prapannānām; bhaya; apaham; āpṛcche; śāpa; nirmuktaḥ; pāda; sparśāt; amīva; han
verses.purport: According to the ācāryas, the word āpṛcche indicates that Sudarśana humbly requested the Lord for permission to return to his abode, where he might take up his duties again, certainly in a chastened state of mind.

verses.text: प्रपन्नोऽस्मि महायोगिन् महापुरुष सत्पते ।अनुजानीहि मां देव सर्वलोकेश्वरेश्वर ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: prapanno ’smi mahā-yoginmahā-puruṣa sat-pateanujānīhi māṁ devasarva-lokeśvareśvara
verses.meaning: O master of mystic power, O great personality, O Lord of the devotees, I surrender to You. Please command me as You will, O supreme God, Lord of all lords of the universe.
verses.wordMeanings: prapannaḥ; asmi; mahā; yogin; mahā; puruṣa; sat; pate; anujānīhi; mām; deva; sarva; loka; īśvara; īśvara

verses.text: ब्रह्मदण्डाद्विमुक्तोऽहं सद्यस्तेऽच्युत दर्शनात् ।यन्नाम गृह्णन्नखिलान् श्रोतृनात्मानमेव च ।सद्य: पुनाति किं भूयस्तस्य स्पृष्ट: पदा हि ते ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahma-daṇḍād vimukto ’haṁsadyas te ’cyuta darśanātyan-nāma gṛhṇann akhilānśrotṝn ātmānam eva casadyaḥ punāti kiṁ bhūyastasya spṛṣṭaḥ padā hi te
verses.meaning: O infallible one, I was immediately freed from the brāhmaṇas’ punishment simply by seeing You. Anyone who chants Your name purifies all who hear his chanting, as well as himself. How much more beneficial, then, is the touch of Your lotus feet?
verses.wordMeanings: brahma; daṇḍāt; vimuktaḥ; aham; sadyaḥ; te; acyuta; darśanāt; yat; nāma; gṛhṇan; akhilān; śrotṝn; ātmānam; eva; ca; sadyaḥ; punāti; kim; bhūyaḥ; tasya; spṛṣṭaḥ; padā; hi; te

verses.text: इत्यनुज्ञाप्य दाशार्हं परिक्रम्याभिवन्द्य च ।सुदर्शनो दिवं यात: कृच्छ्रान्नन्दश्च मोचित: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity anujñāpya dāśārhaṁparikramyābhivandya casudarśano divaṁ yātaḥkṛcchrān nandaś ca mocitaḥ
verses.meaning: Thus receiving the permission of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the demigod Sudarśana circumambulated Him, bowed down to offer Him homage and then returned to his heavenly planet. Nanda Mahārāja was thus delivered from peril.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; anujñāpya; dāśārham; parikramya; abhivandya; ca; sudarśanaḥ; divam; yātaḥ; kṛcchrāt; nandaḥ; ca; mocitaḥ

verses.text: निशाम्य कृष्णस्य तदात्मवैभवंव्रजौकसो विस्मितचेतसस्तत: ।समाप्य तस्मिन् नियमं पुनर्व्रजंनृपाययुस्तत् कथयन्त आद‍ृता: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśāmya kṛṣṇasya tad ātma-vaibhavaṁvrajaukaso vismita-cetasas tataḥsamāpya tasmin niyamaṁ punar vrajaṁṇṛpāyayus tat kathayanta ādṛtāḥ
verses.meaning: The inhabitants of Vraja were astonished to see the mighty power of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Dear King, they then completed their worship of Lord Śiva and returned to Vraja, along the way respectfully describing Kṛṣṇa’s powerful acts.
verses.wordMeanings: niśāmya; kṛṣṇasya; tat; ātma; vaibhavam; vraja; okasaḥ; vismita; cetasaḥ; tataḥ; samāpya; tasmin; niyamam; punaḥ; vrajam; nṛpa; āyayuḥ; tat; kathayantaḥ; ādṛtāḥ

verses.text: कदाचिदथ गोविन्दो रामश्चाद्भ‍ुतविक्रम: ।विजह्रतुर्वने रात्र्यां मध्यगौ व्रजयोषिताम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: kadācid atha govindorāmaś cādbhuta-vikramaḥvijahratur vane rātryāṁmadhya-gau vraja-yoṣitām
verses.meaning: Once Lord Govinda and Lord Rāma, the performers of wonderful feats, were playing in the forest at night with the young girls of Vraja.
verses.wordMeanings: kadācit; atha; govindaḥ; rāmaḥ; ca; adbhuta; vikramaḥ; vijahratuḥ; vane; rātryām; madhya; gau; vraja; yoṣitām
verses.purport: This verse introduces a new pastime. According to the ācāryas, the occasion mentioned here is the Holikā-pūrṇimā, a day also known as Gaura-pūrṇimā.

verses.text: उपगीयमानौ ललितं स्त्रीजनैर्बद्धसौहृदै: ।स्वलङ्कृतानुलिप्ताङ्गौ स्रग्विनौ विरजोऽम्बरौ ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: upagīyamānau lalitaṁstrī-janair baddha-sauhṛdaiḥsv-alaṅkṛtānuliptāṅgausragvinau virajo-’mbarau
verses.meaning: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma wore flower garlands and spotless garments, and Their limbs were beautifully decorated and anointed. The women sang Their glories in a charming way, bound to Them by affection.
verses.wordMeanings: upagīyamānau; lalitam; strī; janaiḥ; baddha; sauhṛdaiḥ; su; alaṅkṛta; anulipta; aṅgau; srak; vinau; virajaḥ; ambarau

verses.text: निशामुखं मानयन्तावुदितोडुपतारकम् ।मल्लिकागन्धमत्तालि जुष्टं कुमुदवायुना ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśā-mukhaṁ mānayantāvuditoḍupa-tārakammallikā-gandha-mattāli-juṣṭaṁ kumuda-vāyunā
verses.meaning: The two Lords praised the nightfall, signaled by the rising of the moon and the appearance of stars, a lotus-scented breeze and bees intoxicated by the fragrance of jasmine flowers.
verses.wordMeanings: niśā; mukham; mānayantau; udita; uḍupa; tārakam; mallikā; gandha; matta; ali; juṣṭam; kumuda; vāyunā

verses.text: जगतु: सर्वभूतानां मन:श्रवणमङ्गलम् ।तौ कल्पयन्तौ युगत्स्वरमण्डलमूर्च्छितम् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: jagatuḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁmanaḥ-śravaṇa-maṅgalamtau kalpayantau yugapatsvara-maṇḍala-mūrcchitam
verses.meaning: Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma sang, producing the entire range of musical sounds simultaneously. Their singing brought happiness to the ears and minds of all living beings.
verses.wordMeanings: jagatuḥ; sarva; bhūtānām; manaḥ; śravaṇa; maṅgalam; tau; kalpayantau; yugapat; svara; maṇḍala; mūrcchitam

verses.text: गोप्यस्तद्गीतमाकर्ण्य मूर्च्छिता नाविदन्नृप ।स्रंसद्दुकूलमात्मानं स्रस्तकेशस्रजं तत: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopyas tad-gītam ākarṇyamūrcchitā nāvidan nṛpasraṁsad-dukūlam ātmānaṁsrasta-keśa-srajaṁ tataḥ
verses.meaning: The gopīs became stunned when they heard that song. Forgetting themselves, O King, they did not notice that their fine garments were becoming loose and their hair and garlands disheveled.
verses.wordMeanings: gopyaḥ; tat; gītam; ākarṇya; mūrcchitāḥ; na; avidan; nṛpa; sraṁsat; dukūlam; ātmānam; srasta; keśa; srajam; tataḥ

verses.text: एवं विक्रीडतो: स्वैरं गायतो: सम्प्रमत्तवत् ।शङ्खचूड इति ख्यातो धनदानुचरोऽभ्यगात् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ vikrīḍatoḥ svairaṁgāyatoḥ sampramatta-vatśaṅkhacūḍa iti khyātodhanadānucaro ’bhyagāt
verses.meaning: While Lord Kṛṣṇa and Lord Balarāma thus played according to Their own sweet will and sang to the point of apparent intoxication, a servant of Kuvera named Śaṅkhacūḍa came upon the scene.
verses.wordMeanings: evam; vikrīḍatoḥ; svairam; gāyatoḥ; sampramatta; vat; śaṅkhacūḍaḥ; iti; khyātaḥ; dhana; da; anucaraḥ; abhyagāt

verses.text: तयोर्निरीक्षतो राजंस्तन्नाथं प्रमदाजनम् ।क्रोशन्तं कालयामास दिश्युदीच्यामशङ्कित: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayor nirīkṣato rājaṁstan-nāthaṁ pramadā-janamkrośantaṁ kālayām āsadiśy udīcyām aśaṅkitaḥ
verses.meaning: O King, even as the two Lords looked on, Śaṅkhacūḍa brazenly began driving the women off toward the north. The women, who had accepted Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma as their Lords, began to cry out to Them.
verses.wordMeanings: tayoḥ; nirīkṣatoḥ; rājan; tat; nātham; pramadā; janam; krośantam; kālayām; āsa; diśi; udīcyām; aśaṅkitaḥ
verses.purport: According to Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī, the demon Śaṅkhacūḍa shook a large stick at the beautiful young girls, thus frightening them and driving them toward the north. He did not actually touch them, as is corroborated by the following verse.

verses.text: क्रोशन्तं कृष्ण रामेति विलोक्य स्वपरिग्रहम् ।यथा गा दस्युना ग्रस्ता भ्रातरावन्वधावताम् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: krośantaṁ kṛṣṇa rāmetivilokya sva-parigrahamyathā gā dasyunā grastābhrātarāv anvadhāvatām
verses.meaning: Hearing Their devotees crying out “Kṛṣṇa! Rāma!” and seeing that they were just like cows being stolen by a thief, Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma began to run after the demon.
verses.wordMeanings: krośantam; kṛṣṇa; rāma; iti; vilokya; sva; parigraham; yathā; gāḥ; dasyunā; grastāḥ; bhrātarau; anvadhāvatām

verses.text: मा भैष्टेत्यभयारावौ शालहस्तौ तरस्विनौ ।आसेदतुस्तं तरसा त्वरितं गुह्यकाधमम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: mā bhaiṣṭety abhayārāvauśāla-hastau tarasvinauāsedatus taṁ tarasātvaritaṁ guhyakādhamam
verses.meaning: The Lords called out in reply, “Do not fear!” Then They picked up logs of the śala tree and quickly pursued that lowest of Guhyakas, who swiftly ran away.
verses.wordMeanings: mā; bhaiṣṭa; iti; abhaya; ārāvau; śāla; hastau; tarasvinau; āsedatuḥ; tam; tarasā; tvaritam; guhyaka; adhamam

verses.text: स वीक्ष्य तावनुप्राप्तौ कालमृत्यू इवोद्विजन् ।विषृज्य स्त्रीजनं मूढ: प्राद्रवज्जीवितेच्छया ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vīkṣya tāv anuprāptaukāla-mṛtyū ivodvijanviṣṛjya strī-janaṁ mūḍhaḥprādravaj jīvitecchayā
verses.meaning: When Śaṅkhacūḍa saw the two of Them coming toward him like the personified forces of Time and Death, he was filled with anxiety. Confused, he abandoned the women and fled for his life.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; vīkṣya; tau; anuprāptau; kāla; mṛtyū; iva; udvijan; visṛjya; strī; janam; mūḍhaḥ; prādravat; jīvita; icchayā

verses.text: तमन्वधावद् गोविन्दो यत्र यत्र स धावति ।जिहीर्षुस्तच्छिरोरत्नं तस्थौ रक्षन् स्त्रियो बल: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tam anvadhāvad govindoyatra yatra sa dhāvatijihīrṣus tac-chiro-ratnaṁtasthau rakṣan striyo balaḥ
verses.meaning: Lord Govinda chased the demon wherever he ran, eager to take his crest jewel. Meanwhile Lord Balarāma stayed with the women to protect them.
verses.wordMeanings: tam; anvadhāvat; govindaḥ; yatra; yatra; saḥ; dhāvati; jihīrṣuḥ; tat; śiraḥ; ratnam; tasthau; rakṣan; striyaḥ; balaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains that the women were fatigued from being driven away, and thus Lord Balarāma protected them and consoled them as they rested. Meanwhile Lord Kṛṣṇa went after the demon.

verses.text: अविदूर इवाभ्येत्य शिरस्तस्य दुरात्मन: ।जहार मुष्टिनैवाङ्ग सहचूडमणिं विभु: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: avidūra ivābhyetyaśiras tasya durātmanaḥjahāra muṣṭinaivāṅgasaha-cūḍa-maṇiṁ vibhuḥ
verses.meaning: The mighty Lord overtook Śaṅkhacūḍa from a great distance as if from nearby, my dear King, and then with His fist the Lord removed the wicked demon’s head, together with his crest jewel.
verses.wordMeanings: avidūre; iva; abhyetya; śiraḥ; tasya; durātmanaḥ; jahāra; muṣṭinā; eva; aṅga; saha; cūḍa; maṇim; vibhuḥ

verses.text: शङ्खचूडं निहत्यैवं मणिमादाय भास्वरम् ।अग्रजायाददात्प्रीत्या पश्यन्तीनां च योषिताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaṅkhacūḍaṁ nihatyaivaṁmaṇim ādāya bhāsvaramagrajāyādadāt prītyāpaśyantīnāṁ ca yoṣitām
verses.meaning: Having thus killed the demon Śaṅkhacūḍa and taken away his shining jewel, Lord Kṛṣṇa gave it to His elder brother with great satisfaction as the gopīs watched.
verses.wordMeanings: śaṅkhacūḍam; nihatya; evam; maṇim; ādāya; bhāsvaram; agra; jāya; adadāt; prītyā; paśyantīnām; ca; yoṣitām
verses.purport: Various gopīs perhaps thought that Govinda would give one of them the valuable jewel. To prevent rivalry among them, Śrī Kṛṣṇa happily gave the jewel to His older brother, Balarāma.
